title.en: Aadhaar Corrections — Name, DOB, Address and Mobile: Limits, Documents and Stuck Cases

answerBox.en: Aadhaar updates have hard limits: name twice in a lifetime, date of birth once, gender once — address and mobile can change any number of times. Address updates work online (₹50); name/DOB/mobile need an enrolment centre visit (₹50–100). Rejections mostly trace to document mismatches; beyond-limit changes need UIDAI regional-office exception processing with strong proof.

en.title: Know the limits before you burn them
en.summary: Name 2×, DOB 1×, gender 1× — plan the target value.
en.detail: UIDAI caps: **name — twice** in a lifetime, **date of birth — once**, **gender — once**; address/mobile/email/photo are unlimited. So fix toward the **final authoritative value** — usually the birth certificate/passport spelling — in one shot, aligned with your other IDs ([birth certificate first](/help/government-paperwork/birth-certificate-correction) where that is the anchor). DOB corrections need a **documentary proof of birth** (birth certificate/passport); "declared age" enrolments converting to documented DOB is precisely what the single chance is for.

en.title: Choose the right channel per field
en.summary: Address online; name/DOB/biometrics at a centre.
en.detail: **Address**: online at **[myAadhaar](https://myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in)** (₹50) with PoA documents, or the **Head-of-Family consent flow** when documents are in a relative's name. **Name/DOB/gender/mobile/biometrics**: book an **enrolment/update centre** (Aadhaar Seva Kendra) appointment — carry originals (scanned back to you), pay ₹50 (demographic) / ₹100 (with biometrics). Track with the URN; download the updated e-Aadhaar (as valid as the card). The 10-yearly **document update** (refresh PoI/PoA) keeps authentications smooth.

en.title: Rejections: read the reason, fix the gap
en.summary: Mismatched documents cause most; affidavits + gazette bridge.
en.detail: Common rejection causes: update-document name differing from the target name (bridge with the **one-and-the-same/name-change affidavit** and, for full name changes, the **[gazette route](/help/family-personal/name-change-process)** — UIDAI accepts gazette notifications as name proof), poor scans, or using non-listed documents (check UIDAI's valid-documents list). Repeated rejections with valid papers: raise a grievance on the UIDAI portal/**1947** helpline with the URNs, then escalate in writing to the **UIDAI Regional Office**.

en.title: Beyond-limit and hard cases
en.summary: Regional-office exception handling; RTI/appeals for silence.
en.detail: Genuine needs beyond the caps (a wrong DOB burned by an earlier bad update, court-ordered name changes) go through **exception handling at the UIDAI Regional Office**: written request, the full document trail, sometimes a court order/gazette — processed case-by-case. Persistent silence yields to an **[RTI](/help/government-paperwork/rti-application-how-to)** on the file's status and the CPGRAMS grievance ladder. For fraud on your Aadhaar (unknown authentications, misuse), see the [identity-theft guide](/help/cyber-fraud/identity-theft-aadhaar-misuse) — locking biometrics is step one.

rows.label: Bridging affidavit / gazette help
rows.note: WakilBhai drafting; gazette per that process

rows.label: e-Aadhaar download
rows.note: Equally valid as the physical card

en.footnote: Aadhaar is downstream of your anchor documents — fix the birth certificate/passport first, then align Aadhaar, then everything else. Fixing Aadhaar to a value your anchors contradict wastes a lifetime cap.

en.q: I've used my one DOB change and it's still wrong. Any hope?
en.a: Yes, via exception handling: a written request to the UIDAI Regional Office with conclusive proof (birth certificate/passport, and where needed a court declaration of DOB) gets case-by-case processing. It is slower and evidence-hungry — but the cap is administrative, not absolute, for genuine documented errors.

en.q: My documents are in my pre-marriage name; Aadhaar should show my new name. Route?
en.a: Marriage certificate is an accepted name-change proof for Aadhaar — carry it with your existing PoI at the centre. For bigger changes (full rename), the affidavit-ads-gazette route gives UIDAI the gazette notification it accepts without argument. This burns one of the two name-change chances — go directly to the final version.

en.q: Can I update a child's Aadhaar?
en.a: Yes — children's biometrics are mandatorily updated at 5 and 15 (free within the window), and demographic corrections follow the same document rules with parent consent. Blue (Bal) Aadhaar for under-5s uses parental authentication. School-record alignment matters before board exams — fix mismatches early.

en.q: Is the mobile number update possible online?
en.a: No — mobile/email changes need a centre visit with biometric verification (that linkage is the OTP security anchor). Address is the online-updatable field. Book the ASK appointment; the update reflects in about a week.

en.q: My address documents are in my father's/landlord's name. How do I update?
en.a: Use the Head-of-Family flow (relative's consent + relationship proof) for family homes; for rentals, a registered rent agreement in your name works as PoA. UIDAI's valid-document list also includes bank statements, insurance, and government letters — one of them usually fits.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
